name: Ruby Pears
recipeIngredient: 1 Bottle Beaujolais Wine or Grape Juice (26 Oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Core the pears from the bottom, keeping the stem intact.
2. Place wine and jelly in a 4- or 6-quart Presto Pressure Cooker; heat and stir to melt the jelly.
3. Pare thin strips of lemon peel using a vegetable peeler; add to the wine mixture.
4. Squeeze lemon juice into the cooker.
5. Cut four pieces of 12-inch aluminum foil.
6. Dip each pear in the hot liquid and place on the aluminum foil squares.
7. Bring up the four corners of the foil over the pears and twist securely, repeating until all pears are wrapped.
8. Add any remaining ingredients.
9. Place the cooking rack into the cooker.
10. Arrange the pears upright on the rack.
11. Close the cover securely.
12. Place the pressure regulator on the vent pipe and cook for 4 to 8 minutes with the regulator rocking slowly.
13. Cool the cooker immediately.
14. Carefully remove the pears and place them in a deep bowl; pour hot liquid over the pears.
15. Allow to cool and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
